name: Valle del Naranco - Valle de Lechada Trail
name_original: Boquerón de las Bobias y Llánaves de la Reina – circular desde Portilla de la Reina por el Parque Regional de Picos de Europa
description: Explore the Valle del Naranco - Valle de Lechada Trail, a moderate 13.6-mile mountain biking route in Picos de Europa Regional Park.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Boca de Huérgano are situated within the Cantabrian Mountains, bordering the Picos de Europa National Park in León, Spain. The region features dramatic mountainous scenery, deeply carved valleys, and lush forests, offering a diverse terrain for mountain biking. This area is characterized by continuous ascents, technical descents, and a mix of multi-use dirt roads and challenging singletrack trails. The landscape provides a natural playground with varied topography suitable for different riding styles. Best mountain bike trails around Boca De Huérgano The most popular mountain bike trail is Boquerón de las Bobias y Llánaves de la Reina – circular desde Portilla de la Reina por el Parque Regional de Picos de Europa, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views within the Picos de Europa Regional Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Barniedo de la Reina – Puerto Picones (1316 m) loop from Burón, a difficult 47.2 miles (76.0 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and panoramic mountain vistas. Local mountain bikers also love the Riaño Dam – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño, a 31.3 miles (50.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with views of the Riaño Dam, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Mountain biking around Boca De Huérgano is defined by the rugged Cantabrian Mountains, the dramatic Picos de Europa, and serene valleys. The extensive network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to technically demanding routes. The routes in Boca De Huérgano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Boca De Huérgano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect around Boca de Huérgano?
A: The region offers a diverse range of mountain biking experiences, from multi-use dirt and gravel roads suitable for All-Mountain (AM), Cross-Country (XC), and Gravel biking, to challenging singletrack trails. You'll find continuous ascents with steep sections, winding roads, and exhilarating downhill stretches, often on natural paths that have been used for centuries.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Boca de Huérgano?
A: While many routes present significant challenges, there are options for moderate rides that can be enjoyed by those with less experience or families. The area's extensive network allows for varying ability levels, though the terrain is generally rugged and mountainous. Currently, there are no trails specifically classified as 'easy' in our selection, but 26 moderate routes offer a good starting point.

Q: What kind of challenges do advanced mountain bikers face in Boca de Huérgano?
A: Advanced riders will find plenty of demanding routes with significant elevation gain, technical descents, and rough terrain. The Cantabrian Mountains provide a natural playground with continuous climbs and winding roads. Some singletracks, especially closer to the Picos de Europa, can be very technical and steep, featuring rock gardens and challenging descents. There are 31 difficult routes available for experienced riders.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking?
A: You'll be treated to spectacular views of the Picos de Europa National Park and the surrounding Cantabrian landscape. Highlights include the dramatic scenery from high mountain passes like San Glorio and Pandetrave. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4643710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pandetrave Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2403778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cirque of the Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also rich in lush forests, serene valleys, and historic paths.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Boca de Huérgano are designed as loops. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037523744/boqueron-de-las-bobias-y-llanaves-de-la-reina-circular-desde-portilla-de-la-reina-por-el-parque-regional-de-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquerón de las Bobias y Llánaves de la Reina – circular desde Portilla de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; offers views within the Picos de Europa Regional Park. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40307750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barniedo de la Reina – Puerto Picones (1316 m) loop from Burón&lt;/a&gt;, featuring panoramic mountain vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Boca de Huérgano?
A: The region's mountainous terrain means that spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions. During these seasons, you can expect more stable weather and accessible trails. Winter riding can be challenging due to snow and ice, especially at higher elevations and passes like San Glorio, which can be closed.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Boca de Huérgano?
A: The trails around Boca de Huérgano are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the stunning scenery, the sense of isolation and tranquility found on many routes, and the varied and challenging terrain that caters to different skill levels. The breathtaking views of the Picos de Europa are a consistent highlight.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while mountain biking in this area?
A: The region's lush forests and remote valleys, particularly bordering the Picos de Europa National Park, are home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for local fauna, including various bird species and potentially larger mammals. The Mirador de Llesba, near San Glorio Pass, even features a statue of a brown bear, indicating the presence of these animals in the wider Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Riaño Dam?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning views of the Riaño Dam. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Dam – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Riaño&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through mountainous terrain with excellent vistas of the dam. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24633510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riaño Swing – Valcayo Viewpoint loop from Boca de Huérgano&lt;/a&gt;, also offers these scenic views.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Boca de Huérgano?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails available around Boca de Huérgano, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels. These include 26 moderate and 31 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every rider seeking adventure in the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: Are there any mountain huts or refuges along the trails?
A: Yes, the region has several huts and refuges that can be found along or near mountain biking routes. These can offer a place to rest or shelter. Notable examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/175952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Urriellu Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2403791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verónica Cabin&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3679564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Áliva Refuge Hotel&lt;/a&gt;, among others.
tips: From this point on the trail, we will have a magnificent panoramic view of the refuge known as Tajahierro with the mountains of the Picos de Europa Regional Park in the background.
Beautiful picture when we find ourselves rolling through a spectacular area of the Cantabrian Mountains along a wide dirt road without any technical difficulty.
Nice picture along the picturesque path (PR-24) between Portillo del Boquerón and the town of Llánaves de la Reina, rolling along the Arroyo del Naranco.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Valle del Naranco - Valle de Lechada Trail by mountain bike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 21.9 kilometers long and generally takes around 2 hours and 15 minutes to complete on a mountain bike. Keep in mind that individual times can vary based on fitness levels and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of mountain paths and dirt tracks. You'll experience a varied landscape, starting with an enclosed section before opening into wide pastures flanked by conglomerate mountains. There's a descent through sparse broom, connecting to a track in the Lechada valley, which features a long descent. Expect areas of magnificent glacial relief with wide meadow bottoms and steep heather and broom slopes.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del Naranco - Valle de Lechada Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate difficulty. While suitable for cycle tourism, some sections might involve more strenuous climbs or descents. It's best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What natural features or wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of wide pastures, powerful conglomerate mountains, and areas of glacial relief. The area is a significant biological corridor for brown bears in the eastern Cantabrian Mountains. In May, you might witness the spectacular flowering of the protected narcissus (capilote).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly notable in May for the spectacular flowering of the protected narcissus (capilote), making it a beautiful time to visit. Generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting points. You'll encounter the Tajahierro Amalio Fernández Refuge and the Portillo del Boquerón. A section of the PR-24 Trail: Portillo del Boquerón to Llánaves de la Reina is also part of this route.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the PR LE-22 (Valle del Naranco-Lechada and Camino de Lechada), the route also intersects with sections of the Puerto de San Glorio, Camino Vadiniense, Arroyo de Orpiñas, and PR-23 trails.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del Naranco - Valle de Lechada Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like brown bears and protected flora. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the trail or the Picos de Europa Regional Park?
faqs.answer: The Valle del Naranco - Valle de Lechada Trail is located within the Picos de Europa Regional Park. Generally, there are no specific entrance fees or permits required for hiking or mountain biking on established trails within the park. However, always check the official park regulations for any updates, especially concerning specific activities or protected areas.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, so you can start at various points. For specific parking information, it's best to consult local maps or resources for Portilla de la Reina, which is a common starting point for routes in this area.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, appropriate clothing layers for changing mountain weather, a helmet, and a first-aid kit. Sun protection is also advisable.

waypoints.name: PR-24 Trail: Portillo del Boquerón to Llánaves de la Reina
